title: 6 podiatrist-approved sandals to comfortably walk in all summer
summary: Sandals are notoriously not great for lots of walking. These podiatrists-approved sandals have built-in arch support and cushioning to keep you comfortable.
story: Sandal season means trips to the beach, bright pedicures and more fun stuff. But, for many, wearing sandals also means blisters and soreness if you walk long distances in them.

“Thin, loose, or stretchy straps mean your foot has to ‘work’ just to keep the sandal on, so your toes grip, your gait gets shorter and more shuffling, and you start to feel fatigue not just in the foot but up into the knees, hips, and even the low back,” says Dr. Mikel Daniels, president and chief medical officer of We Treat Feet Podiatry.

Thankfully, there are sandals out there that are suitable for walking — you just have to know what to look for. To help you shop, I spoke with Daniels and other podiatrists on what to prioritize to ensure your sandals are comfortable for walking, and got their recommendations on sandals worth your consideration.

Best overallBirkenstock Arizona Sandals$undefined$117.95ZapposLearn moreBest cushioningOofos Ooahh Recovery Slides$undefined$59.95AmazonLearn moreBest flip flopsOluKai Ohana Flip Flops$undefined$79.99AmazonLearn more
Best overallBirkenstock Arizona Sandals$undefined$117.95ZapposLearn more
Best cushioningOofos Ooahh Recovery Slides$undefined$59.95AmazonLearn more
Best flip flopsOluKai Ohana Flip Flops$undefined$79.99AmazonLearn more
The best walking sandals of 2026
To compile this list of the best walking sandals, I gathered recommendations from experts and also used their guidance to find highly-rated options that are worth your consideration.
Birkenstock Arizona Sandals

The Arizona is arguably Birkenstock’s most iconic sandal, but any sandal from the brand will give you solid arch support and a comfortable, durable footbed, says Dr. Tek Fish, podiatrist and owner of Clover Podiatry. I have been wearing these sandals for years. I have wide, flat feet and find that they accommodate larger feet without pinching and are supportive without being cumbersome.

The adjustable straps make it easy to get a customized fit and there is a raised toe bar on the footbed that gives your toes something to grip as you walk, which helps the shoe stay on, according to the brand.
Oofos Ooahh Recovery Slides

Fish also recommends Oofos, saying they are great for recovering after a workout but can also provide all-day comfort. The unisex slide is highly cushioned to absorb impact as you walk and the contoured footbed provides arch support. The shoes are also easy to wipe down and can even be thrown in the washing machine — just don’t put them in the dryer. One thing to note: They do not come in half sizes. If you are a half size, Oofos recommends sizing up if you are a woman and down if you are a man.
OluKai Ohana Flip Flops

“OluKai sandals are a strong choice if you want a cushioned feel and a shape that tends to work better for wider and flatter feet,” says Daniels. “They are comfortable and will help prevent pain in the Achilles area if worn all day.” These flip flops are lightweight, have arch support and a sole with treads for traction. They also have a quick-drying insole in case you get them wet. Though these are made for women, the brand also makes a version for men.
Teva Hurricane XLT 2 Sandal

Experts I spoke with said part of what makes sandals uncomfortable for walking is that you have to clench your toes as you walk to keep them on your feet. Fish recommends these Tevas because they have a strap that runs over the toe area, as well as a strap that secures the sandal to your ankle. This means you don’t have to clench to keep them secure.

The sandals are available for both men and women and come in a variety of colors to suit your vibe. They can be worn in the water or on dry land and the straps are made from quick-drying nylon. The cushioned footbed is contoured to cradle your foot and provide support and the sole has deep treads for traction.
Naot Kayla Women’s Sandals

If you are looking for a slightly dressier (read: less sporty looking) sandal that is still comfortable to walk in, consider these from Naot, says Dr. Samantha Landau, a podiatrist and professor at New York College of Podiatric Medicine at Touro University. They have a slight wedge, but aren’t so high that your feet will get achy. They also have straps across the toes and midfoot, as well as an ankle strap to keep your foot secure.
The insole is cushioned and there is good arch support, according to the brand. The shoes also come in narrow, regular and wide width sizes.
Clarks Men's Walkford Slides

Landau also recommends sandals from Clarks for how supportive and cushioned they are. These slides have a wide band that runs across the midsole, keeping the sandal secure on the foot. The upper is made from leather and the outsole is rubber to provide good traction. There is built-in arch support and the toe box is on the wider side to give feet plenty of room. The shoes are available in regular and wide widths.
How I picked the best walking sandals
To choose the most comfortable sandals for walking, I asked experts what to consider while shopping. Here are the things they suggest you prioritize:

Support: “A flat, flimsy insole is one step above walking barefoot,” says Fish. “You want something that supports your foot — the more a sandal cups your heel to hold your foot in place and has arch support, the better.” One trick to tell if a shoe has enough arch support: “If you fold the sandal in half, it should bend at the ball of the foot only and not in the middle of the foot,” says Dr. Chandana Halaharvi, a foot and ankle surgeon and owner of Thrive Foot and Ankle. “If it folds too easily, like a wallet, you don’t have enough arch support for the shoe and your foot will be working overtime.Cushioning: Look for a cushioned but not mushy midsole. “You want to make sure there is shock absorption, but without losing stability,” says Halaharvi. “If the sole compresses too easily when you step on it, there’s a good chance this is not enough padding for comfortable walking.” Strap security: How well the sandal keeps your foot in place matters. “A sandal that shifts around forces your toes and smaller stabilizing muscles to compensate constantly, which will cause fatigue,” says Fish. “A well-secured foot is a less tired foot.” Look for a sandal with thick straps — ideally that secure your foot in multiple places, like across the midfoot, around the ankle or across the top of the toes.

Support: “A flat, flimsy insole is one step above walking barefoot,” says Fish. “You want something that supports your foot — the more a sandal cups your heel to hold your foot in place and has arch support, the better.” One trick to tell if a shoe has enough arch support: “If you fold the sandal in half, it should bend at the ball of the foot only and not in the middle of the foot,” says Dr. Chandana Halaharvi, a foot and ankle surgeon and owner of Thrive Foot and Ankle. “If it folds too easily, like a wallet, you don’t have enough arch support for the shoe and your foot will be working overtime.

Cushioning: Look for a cushioned but not mushy midsole. “You want to make sure there is shock absorption, but without losing stability,” says Halaharvi. “If the sole compresses too easily when you step on it, there’s a good chance this is not enough padding for comfortable walking.”

Strap security: How well the sandal keeps your foot in place matters. “A sandal that shifts around forces your toes and smaller stabilizing muscles to compensate constantly, which will cause fatigue,” says Fish. “A well-secured foot is a less tired foot.” Look for a sandal with thick straps — ideally that secure your foot in multiple places, like across the midfoot, around the ankle or across the top of the toes.
Frequently asked questions

“Your foot actually has to work a lot harder in a sandal than it does in a regular shoe,” says Fish. “If you walk in a sandal the exact same way you walk in a running shoe, the sandal will fall off your foot. So without even thinking about it, a lot of people curl their toes slightly to grip the sandal and keep it on. You’re now using muscles you don’t normally use, and they’re going to tire out faster than you expect.”

Support is another issue. “A sandal just can’t offer what a running shoe or cross-trainer can. Maybe a little arch support, but that’s about it,” says Fish. That said, there are more supportive sandals that can go a long way towards keeping you comfortable.
Yes, as with any shoe, the more you wear it, the more it will begin to conform to your foot. Not only that, sometimes you just need to get used to wearing sandals in general.

“If you’ve been in boots and shoes all winter and you throw on sandals for a long walk, your feet aren’t going to be happy, not because sandals are bad, but because your feet aren’t used to them,” says Fish. “It’s not a permanent problem. People who wear sandals every day don’t have this issue. But most of us only bust them out in spring or summer, and our feet feel it.”
Meet our experts

At NBC Select, we work with experts with specialized knowledge and authority based on relevant training and/or experience. We also ensure all expert advice and recommendations are made independently and without undisclosed financial conflicts of interest.

title: Gwyneth Paltrow Suggests Using Arugula as a Dairy Substitute on TODAY and the Internet Can’t Leaf It Alone
summary: While appearing on TODAY, Gwyneth Paltrow said she subs in arugula “to avoid dairy” while cooking, and the internet is not having it. See the best reactions.
story: It’s a cooking tip that has people on the internet scratching their heads rather than rolling up their sleeves.

On May 27, Oscar winner Gwyneth Paltrow visited Studio 1A to share her recipe for turkey meatballs. Goop Kitchen’s new location in New York will offer the dish, so Paltrow prepared it for Carson Daly and Savannah Guthrie.
Get The Recipe
“I love being in the kitchen,” Paltrow told them as she chopped mushrooms to include in the meatballs. “I find it very grounding.”
While Paltrow may find cooking grounding, the culinary advice she offered viewers unsettled many of them.
As she added Parmesan cheese to her meatballs, she said, “If you want to avoid dairy, one trick that I do is I dice up arugula and I put it in.”
“Really?” Savannah replied. “That’s weird.”
“It sounds weird, but it kind of adds like a nice texture to it, and it’s delicious,” Paltrow says.
On Instagram, where we posted the video, people had a field day with her unusual tip.
“Is this rage bait,” one user wondered.
“‘Hello police? I need to report a crime?’” wrote another.
“I’ll try it in my coffee right now!” someone else joked.
“When I don’t have milk for my pancake mix, I just add lawn clippings,” quipped one more.

Food Network star and frequent TODAY guest Sunny Anderson had a lot to say about the “Marty Supreme” star’s cooking tip, posting a video of herself enjoying a cup of arugula-topped coffee on Instagram.

“She don’t even believe herself,” Anderson said in her video. “Usually, when someone’s about to tell you a lie, they don’t engage with you visually, and then when the part of the lie they want you to believe comes up, they look you in the eye like they want to convince you of it.”

“I love Gwenny too. I love everybody there,” Anderson added. “I’m just saying, I don’t believe Gwenny. I don’t believe you, baby. You can goop all you want … arugula diced up ain’t never added any dairy vibes to anything.”
While it was mostly an arugula-packed pile-on, there was at least one person who seemed to get what Paltrow was saying.

“It sounds odd but I think it’s out of context,” Italy-based food writer Elizabeth Minchilli wrote. “Instead of adding grated parmigiano, you can absolutely add diced arugula. This will lighten the texture and add flavor and moisture.”

And Goop poked a little fun at the whole situation by posting a photo of a cookie sitting on a cup filled with arugula on Instagram, with a supposed response to Paltrow’s dairy-free hack: “‘i love a cold cup of arugula to dip my cookies in!’”
“Y’all have SOME nerve 😅🤣🤣🤣,” Anderson commented.

title: What Happens to Deborah in the ‘Hacks’ Finale?
summary: What happens to all the characters at the end of “Hacks”? Does Deborah die? Here’s how the show ends and what the creators say.
story: Ava (Hannah Einbinder) and Deborah (Jean Smart) have gone through it all. Rivalries. Partnerships. Stints in Las Vegas and late night. Vitriol and joy. Too many jokes to quote.

The episode opens on a triumph. It seems like everything is turning out well for our heroines. Ava is a showrunner. Deborah is the proud owner of a casino in Las Vegas called the Diva, a turn after the show began with her permanent residency ending.

Side characters have their moments, too. Marty Ghilain (Christopher McDonald) is running the Diva. Marcus (Carl Clemons-Hopkins) got recognized as the business mastermind behind Deborah’s team. Jimmy LuSaque (Paul W. Downs) and Kayla Schaeffer (Megan Stalter) take over Latitude after catching on to her father’s scheme selling the likeness of dead clients to AI companies.
And then: An unexpected twist, one that feels true to life and all the more devastating for it.
What Happens to Deborah Vance?
Deborah, earlier in the season, had a mass removed. In the finale, she says that the the doctors called back with an update: The cancer had spread.

During a conversation with Ava, Deborah lays out her diagnosis and what’s next. Doctors want her to do chemo. That’s not what Deborah envisions for herself. “I don’t want to put myself through that,” she says.
Fresh off her record-breaking show at the Garden, and with her casino open, Deborah feels like she can, well — go.
“You know I like to do things on my own terms ... it’s not going to get any better than this. I want to go out on top,” she says.
Ava doesn’t understand her logic.
“If anyone can beat anything, it’s you,” she says.
But Deborah is set on pursuing assisted suicide in Switzerland. She has one last request of Ava: Join her in Europe for a blowout vacation.

Ava, of course, is distressed and can’t accept Deborah’s diagnosis or her plan. At the last minute, though, she turns up at the airport before Deborah takes off and agrees to her proposition. One last vacation, and then the end.
The two go to Paris and traipse around cafes and have a memorable exchange at the Louvre (“The Mona Lisa? Is mid?”).

Throughout the trip looms a mutual understanding of what is to come. At one point, Deborah considers buying salt and pepper shakers to add to her collection, but she decides not to. Ava seems to zone out on the dance floor.
She then tries to convince Deborah to be part of a clinical trial.
“For someone who always said, ‘Listen to women,’ you’re having a hard time hearing me,” Deborah responds, explaining she doesn’t want to “waste away.”
Ava is angry and bereft. She resorts to begging to Deborah not to leave. “I’m sorry,” Deborah says.
After a trip to a church, Ava has a change of heart — sort of.
“I hate this. But I support you. What am I going to do without you?” she says.
Deborah reassures her. “You’ll be OK. You’re a big, brave girl,” she says.
And, after all these years of making fun of Ava’s big hands, she reveals her hands are bigger.
Does Deborah Choose to Die?
It seems like she’s set. The plan is in place. They’re sitting at the train station.

And then — they start brainstorm jokes about the best and worst parts of dying. It’s then that Deborah seems to lose a bit of her momentum. Ava takes the bags and pummels forward to make the train. Deborah stops her. She’s landed on the perfect joke.
“The worst part abut dying is that I can’t even enjoy being bone thin,” she says.
Deborah, after all that, changes her mind, and seems to land on her next (and maybe last) project. “I may not have 30 years. But I think I have another hour,” she says. “Will you help me write it?”

Ava, in tears, hugs her. “Of course,” she says. They start workshopping jokes about cancer in Paris. The show transitions to them walking and talking on the Las Vegas strip, indicating the work keeps going. They’re going to do another special together, even if it’s the last one.
The show ends on that image as Barbra Streisand’s rendition of “Happy Days Are Here Again” plays. The show leaves Deborah’s fate in the air. Instead, it focuses on their partnership.
What Did the ‘Hacks’ Creators Say About the Ending?
“Hacks” creators say the ending was planned far in advance.
“We always knew that this was the ending, so we’ve been building to it for quite some time,” series co-creator Jen Statsky told TheWrap.
Deborah’s decision to stay with Ava and keep writing is in line with the point of the show and a callback to the connection they found with each other in Season 1.

“When these two people meet, they have this creative spark between them that lights them up and changes them ... at the end of the day, that is what she decides is worth living for, and that was always the intention and the point of the show,” Statsky told TheWrap.
Co-creator Lucia Aniello, in an interview with Variety, said the show is meant to end the way it begins.

“Deborah being inspired by her banter with Ava is obviously the inciting incident to the pilot,” she said, “and so, to have it also be the way the show ends is why this was always the idea from the beginning.”
Aniello added that there was never a version of the story where Deborah died because that wasn’t the message they wanted to send.

“The purpose of having her be sick was for the ultimate redemption, the idea of the comedy and writing together saving her life,” Aniello told Variety. “We wouldn’t have had her die. There’s no reason for her to be sick, except to tell the story of how, in the end, she is saved by her want to continue to write.”

title: Joan Cusack Poses on Red Carpet for the First Time Since 2015 for ‘Toy Story 5’
summary: Joan Cusack made her first red carpet appearance since 2015 at the London premiere of “Toy Story 5.”
story: Joan Cusack is back in the spotlight.
The two-time Academy award nominee made her first red carpet appearance since 2015 at the May 28 London premiere of “Toy Story 5.”

Cusack, who reprises her role in the film as the voice of the cowgirl toy Jessie, wore a long black skirt with a white button-down shirt and black glasses. She was accompanied at the event by her husband, Richard Burke.

Cusack, who won an Emmy Award for her work in “Shameless” in 2015, has kept a relatively low profile in recent years. She last appeared onscreen in three episodes of the TV series “Homecoming” in 2020. Her last film appearance onscreen in a movie was 2019’s “Let It Snow.”
Jessie takes center stage in “Toy Story 5,” which hits theaters June 19. In it, Jessie is focused on helping 8-year-old Bonnie find new friends.
“This is Jessie’s story,” she told Britain’s Virgin Radio UK on May 28 in a joint interview with co-star Tim Allen. “It’s glorious.”
“This woman comes through,” Allen said.
Cusack, 63, had long been one of Hollywood’s most bankable supporting comedy actors, securing Oscar nods for “In & Out” and “Working Girl.”

She’s also worked extensively with her brother, John Cusack, appearing alongside him in films like “Sixteen Candles,” “Say Anything,” “High Fidelity” and “Grosse Pointe Blank.” In addition, she’s had roles in “School of Rock,” “Broadcast News,” “Addams Family Values” and “Runaway Bride,” as well as headlining her own short-lived sitcom, “What About Joan?”
Her brother has said his sister always adds to his films.

“She just comes on the set and, you know, like Wile E. Coyote, just runs you over and you’re just like a flat pancake,” he told TODAY’s Willie Geist in 2020. “And then everyone’s really happy. It’s usually the funniest scenes in the movie.”

title: What’s Going on With the Transitional Blush Chatter on TikTok
summary: Celebrity makeup artists Ngozi Esther Edeme, of Painted by Esther, and Patrick Ta, of Patrick Ta Beauty, have weighed in on a recent transitional blush technique.
story: Who originated the “transitional blush” makeup trend? TikTok users have some opinions.

Recently a controversy has emerged in the makeup artist community as some social media users have accused Patrick Ta, founder of Patrick Ta Beauty, of profiting off a makeup technique seemingly popularized by Ngozi Esther Edeme, of Painted by Esther.
Chatter around the two celebrity makeup artists began when Ta shared a video on the social media platform of his new Transition Blurring Blush Duo product.
So, what is transitional blush and who invited the technique? And what have Edeme and Ta said in response to the online conversation? Learn more about the transitional blush controversy, below.
What Is Transitional Blush?

Transitional blush is a makeup technique that seamlessly blends concealer and blush, layering a variety of shades and products to obtain a lifted, soft contoured look. The ending result is a powerful and playful pop of pink on one’s cheeks.
Edeme has been credited for recently popularizing the look, most notably her work with “Love Island USA” Season 7 star Olandria Carthen, Raye, Doechii, Tyla and more.
While she does not take credit for creating the technique, fans took issue with Ta launching products that help others achieve the transitional blush look.
Edeme has shared that she’s been inspired by artists like Kevyn Aucoin, Danessa Myricks and Pat McGrath who are known for their blush applying techniques.
Transitional Blush Controversy Explained
In a May 19 TikTok, Ta said he “created transition blush” to give people the “flawless, seamless, blurred blush application.
In a tutorial posted on May 22, Ta shared how people can use the products he launched to re-create the popular look.
“Have you ever heard of sunset blush? I created that perfect transition blush to give you that golden hour sunkiss effect,” he said.

But the technique looked familiar to some, who called Ta out in the comments. They accused him of replicating a style that Edeme has become known for, taking offense for him using words like “created” and “transitional blush.”
“You mean Painted by Esther created it,” one person commented.
Another said, “So basically the painted by Esther technique.”
A third asked, “Why not collaborate with paintedbyester because this not your original idea?”

But others disagreed and supported Ta, like one who commented, “Guys no one created this . We been layering blushes with other blushes for forever ! He just created a product just for that . This generation is killing me.”

In his May 25 TikTok of him showing off the product, one person commented: “notice how he keeps explaining WHY he made these products and he’s talking about HIS products. never did he say MY technique.. like pls leave this man alone.”
What Ngozi Esther Edeme, of Painted by Esther, Said
On May 24, Edeme shared her thoughts on the situation in a lengthy TikTok. “I’ll only speak about this once,” she captioned the video.

“My goal is to constantly reference Kevyn Aucoin, Danessa, Pat (McGrath), how they move with grace... and intentionality,” she explained in the video, adding that she hasn’t claimed ownership over any technique she is known for. “I am an artist. My driving force is to create and share it. My end goal is to teach and spread my knowledge.”
She later spoke about her experience as a Black woman in the beauty space.

“It’s already hard being Black and being a woman in this industry,” she continued. “You have to fight twice as hard to get just half of what you deserve. “I will reiterate: I did not start anything. I am 29 years old. That would be ludicrous to claim ownership of anything. But what you will not belittle is my influence.”
What Patrick Ta Said
Ta also weighed in on May 24. He uploaded another transition blush tutorial video that he said was filmed March 5.

He addressed the controversy in the caption, writing, “I started working on transition blush 1 and half years ago. I wanted to create a product that was specifically made for this blush look. I do not own this look.”

Ta specifically mentioned and tagged Edeme before adding, “She Is Amazing and so talented. she popularized this look through her work and through Olandria.  i also have been doing this blush look since 2021. Not the same as Esther but my own version.”
He maintained that he did not copy Edeme. “I am a makeup artist. For context I filmed this video on march 5th. I have nothing but love and respect for Esther,” he concluded.
TODAY.com reached out to Edeme and Ta for comment.

title: ‘The Four Seasons’ Season 2 Ending Explained by Tina Fey, Will Forte and the Show’s Creators
summary: What happens at the end of Season 2 of “The Four Seasons”? A recap of Season 2 of “The Four Season” and where every character leaves off.
story: Spoilers ahead for Season 1 and 2 of “The Four Seasons.”
Season 2 of “The Four Seasons” once again touches on the ever-changing dynamics between friends and partners.
From difficult conversations about adult loneliness, deciding to have a child or not, and what long-term marriages should look like, the Netflix comedy balances humor with heartache.

“Part of the show is about these long-term friendships and also long marriages,” co-creator, co-showrunner and writer Tracey Wigfield tells TODAY.com. “It was exciting to not tell love stories that are at the very beginning but decades in and talk about what marriage is really like. It’s not just boring and monotonous, sometimes it is, but it’s also funny and poignant and a million other things.”
Lang Fisher, co-creator, co-showrunner and writer, agrees, adding that aging and being empty nesters is a topic they really wanted to dig in.
“Sometimes in your 50s or 60s you are like, ‘Well, what is next? Is there a next? What do I do and who am I now?’” Fisher says.

The end of Season 1 left viewers with an unexpected development. Nick (Steve Carell), who was married to and divorcing Anne (Kerri Kenney-Silver), was dating the much-younger Ginny (Erika Henningsen). After his sudden death in a car accident, his friends and Anne found out that Ginny was pregnant.

Season 2 begins with the group of friends, including Ginny, on a trip upstate to scatter Nick’s ashes on a mountaintop. Over their multiple failed attempts at having the moment be perfect, viewers learn that Kate (Tina Fey) and Jack (Will Forte) continue to have marital issues and Claude (Marco Calvani) and Danny (Colman Domingo) are at odds about having a child at their age. Anne and Nick’s divorce was not finalized and she was left with all his money, meaning Ginny wouldn’t get any child support.
But there was more to the story, with each individual character internalizing feelings, lying to themselves about what they really want and more.
Read on for what happens at the end of “The Four Seasons” Season 3 and where the characters leave off.
Kate and Jack

Throughout the season, Jack has been committed to keeping his friend group intact and continuing their traditions despite the fact that everyone’s priorities are shifting. In a somewhat depressive and low mood, his behavior strikes a nerve with Kate, who only wants the best for her husband. His “wallowing,” as she describes it, also causes tension in their marriage.

Kate discovers that Jack’s issues run deeper and that he’s been smoking weed to help with his anxieties. She also gets upset when her hopes of starting a bed and breakfast with Danny are crushed because he’s moving to Italy.
Once in Italy visiting Claude and Danny, Kate encourages Jack to run a marathon to “turn this year around” and become “a new man.”
He struggles and she helps him cross the finish line, but not before they hash out why they have been so disconnected in their marriage.
“Something’s lurking, you wouldn’t be so obsessed with trying to fix me if there wasn’t something eating you up too,” Jack tells Kate.

She then tells him all her insecurities, which include being empty nesters and feeling like she doesn’t have a purpose. She also expresses feeling like she’s at the pinnacle of a career she’s worked all her life for “and it’s only OK,” as well as being upset that Danny moved, Nick died, aging, the world being a dark place and worried about her and Jack’s marriage failing.
“That was a very powerful moment,” Forte tells TODAY.com about Fey’s final episode monologue.
So will Kate and Jack’s marriage survive and does she still love him?

“She does,” Fey tells TODAY.com. “Her stuff is all her own stuff. We get to know a little tiny bit more about (her insecurities). We find out a tiny bit about how she grew up, and maybe why she’s afraid of big feelings.”
Fisher adds that Kate loves Jack “deeply” but are “just in a tough moment. “They fight really hard because they love each other so much,” she continues, adding that Kate’s go-to emotion is anger.

“But really, what she wants is for her husband to be (and feel) OK and she feels powerless,” Fisher says, adding that Kate is also dealing with her own personal issues and not good at expressing her emotions.

The showrunners’ hopes for the end of the season was for viewers to see that Kate and Jack are “two people who deeply love each other” and have a difficult time communicating, but are willing to figure it out.
Danny and Claude
Danny and Claude go through a roller coaster of emotions when deciding if they want to become parents. When one does, the other doesn’t and vice versa. Ultimately, they decide against it.
The two, however, move to Italy, where Claude is happy to be surrounded by his family after being away for so long. Danny has his own issues with the move but wants to make this work for his husband.
While Claude appears to be in control in Italy, Calvani tells TODAY.com that his character is “actually not” and believes he has to step it up to make Danny comfortable.
“He’s trying to fill the void of thinking he’s not enough,” Calvani adds.

When Danny’s mother is hospitalized, he returns to the U.S. and begs her to come to Italy with them so he can take care of her. She refuses. That’s when Marco makes an executive decision to return to the U.S. and help Danny take care of his mother.
Anne and Ginny
Anne begins her journey of self discovery. After being reluctant to include Ginny into their group, she offers Ginny and her baby a place to stay.

Ginny starts off “lying to herself” that she can handle her pregnancy all on her own, Henningsen tells TODAY.com. She soon embraces the help from the friend group and finds a close friendship with Anne.

When Anne becomes a little too hands on with Ginny’s baby, her friends encourage her to meet new people. A one-night stand with a guy they met during their vacation, Mark Brett, results in a comical misunderstanding and minor disappointment.

Kenney-Silver tells TODAY.com that in the beginning of Season 2, Anne is lost and keeps pushing along “with this complete terror and complete inability to complete the tasks that she needs to get to the next stage” of her life.
By the end, Anne forces Ginny to move out of her house so that she can have her own life with her baby and have room to grow. Anne also decides to stay in Italy and housesit Claude and Danny’s home.
Officially dubbing herself “Anne Classic,” the season ends with her meeting a man named Gianpiero (David Tennant). Could there be a real love interest in Anne’s future?
Will There Be a Season 3 of ‘The Four Seasons’?
Netflix has not renewed “The Four Seasons” for a third season, but the cast and creators are hopeful.
Wigfield tells TODAY.com that with the “romantic cliffhanger,” she would love to see Anne have “a real love story that doesn’t go wrong right away.”
They also have ideas and “conversations that are interesting for all the characters that we hope we get to do more.”
Fey hopes that the cast and crew “travel some more,” while Forte is game for anything. “It is a dream job,” he says, adding, “I would do whatever.”

title: This Singer Just Confirmed She Is Attending Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce’s Wedding
summary: Ever since Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce announced their engagement, fans have been clamoring to know more about when the happy couple will say “I do.” One celeb has revealed they are on the guest list.
story: Ever since Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce announced their engagement, fans have been clamoring to know when the happy couple will say “I do.”

But Swifties are also curious to know which famous friends will be a part of the celebration and attend. Now, one actor and singer has revealed that she has been invited to Swift and Kelce’s special day.
In an interview with Variety, Suki Waterhouse briefly touched on her engagement with Robert Pattinson, before sharing that she is going to Swift and Kelce’s wedding.

“Nothing scarier than the actual planning part, right?” Waterhouse said, referring to her possible nuptials. “I’m gonna go to Taylor’s wedding, and maybe I’ll get some inspiration. It will be amazing.”
Dating rumors surrounding Waterhouse and Pattinson first popped up in 2018. The two later made their red carpet debut as a couple in 2022. The couple welcomed a daughter together in 2024.

The “Daisy Jones & The Six” star and Swift have been close friends for years. Back in 2023, Waterhouse and Pattinson were photographed out with Swift after attending a screening of Emma Stone’s “Poor Things.”

Then in August 2024, Waterhouse opened for Swift during one of her “Eras Tour” dates in London. In her interview with Variety, the model praised the superstar for supporting musicians and their careers.
“The coolest thing about her is she gives other artists these insane opportunities that really change people’s lives,” Waterhouse said. “Knowing her as a friend, she’s actually been through so much.”
Waterhouse added that she also admires how Swift turns her vulnerability into music.

“The thing I always see in her is how she pulls herself through every time, and how everything that’s ever happened to her becomes another piece of art that becomes part of the legacy,” Waterhouse said. “That’s so inspiring — that’s the artist that you want to be.”

While Waterhouse shared her plans to attend Swift and Kelce’s wedding, other celebrities have been coy. Jack Antonoff, a longtime collaborator and friend of Swift, performed with his band Bleachers on TODAY on May 22 and was asked about his summer plans.
“Are you going to any big weddings this summer?” Craig Melvin asked, not mentioning, but alluding to, Swift and Kelce.

“I hope. Yeah, I hope so, man,” he replied, not touching on any specific weddings he might be attending. Antonoff then turned the conversation around. “I love being married. I’m happy you love it, too,” he said.

title: We’ve Got a Text! Meet the ‘Love Island USA’ Season 8 Cast
summary: The official cast for “Love Island USA” Season 8 has been announced. See who will be competing and looking for love in the villa.
story: Ariana Madix’s got a text! And it has the grand reveal of the “Love Island USA” Season 8 islanders.

As the premiere date for the new season of the hit Peacock reality series approaches, fans have been eagerly awaiting the names of the islanders that they’ll watch fall in love, start drama and meet new bombshells all summer long. On May 28, host Madix announced the 12 hopeful singles moving into the Fijian villa in a video.
“A fresh wave of islanders is moving into the villa, with a high chance of charm and flirtation,” she teased before each cast member made their cheeky introduction.
Among the islanders are the daughter of a former NBA player, the brother of Season 7 British bombshell Charlie Georgiou and a Paralympian.

Fans will get to see the cast meet for the first time when Season 8 premieres on Peacock Tuesday, June 2, at 6 p.m. PT/ 9 p.m. PT. New episodes will then arrive every day except Wednesday at the same time.

Returning this year is host Madix, along with narrator and comedian Iain Stirling. They’ll be joined by “Summer House” star Ciara Miller and media personality Tefi Pessoa, who are taking over as the “Aftersun” hosts. The aftershow, featuring never-before-seen footage and interviews with the dumped islanders, will air every Sunday starting June 13 at 6 p.m. PT/ 9 p.m. ET.
Plus, this season during “Aftersun” episodes Miller and Pessoa will be revealing the identities of the bombshells before they make their dramatic entrances into the villa.
So, who are the original islanders who are ready to begin the summer of a lifetime? Get to know the 12 singles entering the villa, below!
Then, check back here every episode to find out who is still looking for love on Season 8 of “Love Island USA” and who has been dumped from the villa.
The Cast of ‘Love Island USA’ Season 8: Meet the OGs
Aniya Harvey
Current city: Tyrone, Georgia
Age: 23
Occupation: Marketing
Instagram: aniyaharvey02
Aniya, the daughter of a retired NBA player, says she is “a bit competitive” but still friendly. “I’m so freaking extra. Please ‘Love Island,’ give me a man!” she shouts.
Beatriz Hatz
Current city: San Diego, California
Age: 25
Occupation: Professional athlete
Instagram: bhatz_track
Beatriz was born with a “rare birth defect,” she says. But that hasn’t stopped her from becoming a Paralympian. Her type on paper? “Tall, dark and handsome.”
Bryce Dettloff
Current city: Los Angeles, California
Age: 29
Occupation: DJ
Instagram: brycealakai

Bryce says he loves music but he also works as a handyman. “I do electrical work on garbage trucks sometimes. It’s literally so disgusting but it’s like, you’ve got to do what you’ve got to do,” he says.
Gabriel Vasconcelos
Current city: Miami, Florida
Age: 26
Occupation: Model
Instagram: gvasconcelosv
Gabriel wants to bring the “Brazilian fire” to Fiji. He speaks three languages: Portuguese, Spanish and English. But, he adds that making out is his love language.
KC Chandler
Current city: Fresno, California
Age: 23
Occupation: Nursing assistant
Instagram: kc.thagreat
KC is a nursing assistant who says “women love men in healthcare.” He adds, “If you’re man and you have scrubs, your muscles showing a little bit, they are going for that every time.”
Kenzie Annis
Current city: Kennesaw, Georgia
Age: 24
Occupation: Nursing school graduate
Instagram: kenzieannis

Kenzie received her degree from nursing school two days before heading to the villa. “I want a huge family, five to 10 kids. I know that’s kind of crazy!” she says, adding that she would also like to adopt.
Melanie Moreno
Current city: Los Angeles, California
Age: 24
Occupation: Bikini store manager
Instagram: melanieelisa
Melanie is ready to bring her Dominican flare into the villa. Currently, she says the bikinis in the “damaged bin” of her store describe her love life. Hopefully things turn around this summer.
Sean Reifel
Current city: Easton, Pennsylvania
Age: 29
Occupation: Police officer
Instagram: parm3_sean
“You could be having the worst day of your life and I’ll just help you sift through that,” Sean says in his introduction package. He wants a woman who is thoughtful.
Sincere Rhea
Current city: Cape May, New Jersey
Age: 25
Occupation: Athlete
Instagram: sincere.rhea
Sincere is a big fan of his name and says it helps him with pickup lines. “I like to be cheesy. I often end my emails, like, ‘Sincerely Sincere.’” He also says he likes the chase.
Trinity Tatum
Current city: Newport News, Virginia
Age: 22
Occupation: Model
Instagram: soootrini
Trinity is from a small town where she says that dating a second cousin could be a possibility. “So, I just want a fresh start,” she shares. “I love a respectful man.”
Vasana Montgomery
Current city: Beaverton, Oregon
Age: 25
Occupation: Business owner
Instagram: vanity_by_vasana
Vasana says she is the “full package.” She says a world map would describe her type.
Zach Georgiou
Current city: Birmingham, England
Age: 26
Occupation: Digital creator
Instagram: zachgeorgio

Zach is the brother of Charlie Georgiou, who was a bombshell in Season 7. Zach says he is here to see if he “can do better” during his time on the show. He has a cat named Lolo and is looking for someone who is a cat person.

title: Halle Berry Misheard Jenna Bush Hager’s Question and the Reactions Are Priceless
summary: A miscommunication between Halle Berry and Jenna Bush Hager made for a hilarious moment on TODAY with Jenna & Sheinelle Jones.
story: A miscommunication between Halle Berry and Jenna Bush Hager caused Sheinelle Jones  — and practically the entire set — to burst into laughter.
The Oscar winner was a guest on TODAY with Jenna & Sheinelle Thursday, May 28, and shared her opinion on a variety of trending topics during the “Pink Chair Questionnaire” segment.
Jenna saved her best question for last, yielding an unexpected response from Berry. She asked the actor to reveal her “biggest ick.”
But Berry misheard her and thought the TODAY co-host was inquiring about a NSFW word instead.
Stunned, Berry turned to Jenna in disbelief. Sheinelle, who also appeared to mishear her co-host, repeated the question, clarifying that they wanted Berry to share a turn off or pet peeve.
Berry instantly covered her face with her hands and doubled over with laughter before looking shocked again.
“Ooh Halle!” Sheinelle playfully said and pretended to swat Berry with her note cards. “You are naughty!”
The camera slowly zoomed in on Jenna, who looked equally surprised as crew members could be heard chuckingly behind the scenes.
“It sounded like that didn’t it?” Berry asked. “It did to me!”
She then decided to answer the question she misheard. “I was going to say Van’s,” she added, referring to fiancé and musician Van Hunt.
Sheinelle continued to laugh and clap her hands. Jenna, while fanning herself, chimed in and said, “There’s something about the word ‘biggest’ with the word ‘ick,’” explaining the confusion.

Jenna pointed out that the broadcast was on a five-second delay in case there were any slip-ups. “Between, ‘Heated Rivalry’ and biggest ick,” Jenna said as Berry let out a scream, mishearing the co-host once again. (Berry previously mentioned that she wanted to start watching the sports drama).
“Ick! I said ick!” Jenna assured her.
Sheinelle hilariously turned to Berry and joked, “No, she didn’t.”
The segment ended with the three ladies still unable to control their laughter.

title: Author Elle Kennedy Reacts to ‘Off Campus’ Season 2’s Leads
summary: “Off Campus” Season 2 has named its lead couple: Mika Abdalla’s Allie Hayes and Stephen Kalyn’s Dean Di Laurentis.
story: “Off Campus” Season 2 has named its lead couple: Mika Abdalla’s Allie Hayes and Stephen Kalyn’s Dean Di Laurentis.

In a departure from the order of Elle Kennedy’s book series of the same name, Allie and Dean’s love story will be the focal point of Season 2, Prime Video announced May 28, meaning the plot of “Off Campus” Book 3, “The Score,” will leapfrog Book 2, “The Mistake,” led by John Logan.

Viewers of the show may not be surprised by this; the seeds of Allie and Dean’s relationship were planted in a surprise addition to Season 1. At the end of Episode 5, it’s revealed that after Allie’s breakup with longterm boyfriend Sean, she and Dean struck a fling in secret. Episode 6 rewinds the clock and shows how that came to be.
This lines up with the plot of “The Score.” About half of that book follows Allie and Dean’s secret affair. But author Elle Kennedy reassures viewers: “You haven’t seen that much” — yet.
“So half the book, they are hooking up, but once their relationship kind of deepens ... there are so many plot points that are happening that we have not seen,” Kennedy tells TODAY.com.
The book explores more of Allie and Dean’s individual arcs, with Allie at a “crossroads” as she decides where she wants to take her acting career, and Dean starting to fall in love with coaching.
“There’s obviously a big spoiler of another big, massive event that happens,” Kennedy says of the book.

“I think they’re going to see some of their favorite moments that haven’t happened yet. There’s some scenes that they’re going to be getting teary-eyed over that are from the books,” she adds. “There’s a lot more to come, I don’t think that the whole story was already told.”
Kennedy previously told the Hollywood Reporter that she received scripts and rough cuts of Season 1 of “Off Campus.” She said her “must-haves” weren’t specific scenes but certain character dynamics.
She describes the dynamic she hopes to see on-screen between Allie and Dean in Season 2 as an unexpected “deep connection.”
“Especially for Dean,” she says. “I think that the majority of ‘The Score’ is more his journey.”
Allie knows more of “who she is,” while Dean has to come to terms with the realities of life.
“I want to see more of both of them finding this deeper connection,” she says. “Dean, especially, realizing the life of Dean is not all sunshine and rainbows, and just growing as a person.”

“How people deal with tragedy and changes, and family issues, and whether her dad likes you or not,” she says. “Things like that, I’d like to see. I mean there’s a couple scenes I would love that would be in there — but I think they will be.”

Logan and Grace fans, don’t fear. Both will appear in Season 2. Antonio Cipriano will reprise his role as John Logan, while India Fowler was cast as Grace, a series regular role, for the show’s sophomore season.

“I think fans of ‘The Mistake’ are still going to be happy, because we have our Grace. We have our Logan. So it’s not like they’re not going to be in the show,” Kennedy says. “Everyone is going to get their moment.”
Showrunner and creator Louisa Levy also confirmed in the May 28 announcement that Season 1 leads, Ella Bright’s Hannah Wells and Belmont Cameli’s Garrett Graham, will be series regulars in Season 2.

“If you fell in love with Hannah and Garrett, don’t worry — they will still be an integral part of our robust ensemble,” Levy said in a statement “We’re looking forward to telling the next chapter of everyone’s story. There’s a lot for fans to look forward to!”

title: Keke Palmer Calls Her Stage Name a ‘Character to Survive My Life.’ Now She’s Re-Introducing Herself
summary: Keke Palmer explains how her name is just a stage name for her career and she wants to “reintroduce myself.”
story: Keke Palmer says her stage name is more than just a credit. It’s a “character.”
The two-time Emmy Award winner, whose birth name is Lauren Keyana Palmer, recently delivered a TED talk in which she explained the origins of her stage name, Keke Palmer.

Palmer said she became the breadwinner of her family as a child actor after moving to California from her hometown of Robbins, Illinois. She landed roles in “The Wool Cap,” “Akeelah and the Bee” and “True Jackson, VP.”

“As the years flew by, I didn’t just perform onstage — I started performing off it, too,” she said. “I began designing a character to survive my life. That character is Keke Palmer — approachable, capable, funny — a small container my full range could exist inside of without overwhelming anyone. And it worked.
“That character has carried me through 23 years in this industry, through childhood fame, the transition into adulthood, through success I could have never imagined,” she added.
However, “The ‘Burbs” star said she couldn’t forget her past.
“I wasn’t trying to be exceptional. I was trying to be reliable,” she said. “I carried the load, not because I had to, but because I couldn’t un-know what was at stake.”

“Somewhere along the way, I started believing I was a thing that saved us,” she continued. “I was Keke Palmer. I built an entire way of moving through the world around staying alert, staying useful, staying on. I was reflexively disembodied, constantly juggling everything thrown at me.”
She said she was on “autopilot” before realizing she needed to learn how to live, not just survive.

“What I want to share with you is that survival can be so effective, you don’t realize when its no longer needed in your life,” she said. “You might think you need to earn more, prove more, secure one more opportunity, collect one more accolade, or just keep moving long enough until you finally feel safe, when, in reality, you don’t need another achievement. You need a break.”
She concluded her talk by resetting just who she is.

“I went to Bali this past year and finally spent some one-on-one time with that little girl who left Robbins, Illinois, all those years ago,” she said. “So, please, allow me to reintroduce myself: My name is Lauren Keyana Palmer, and I’m the CEO of the Keke Palmer Company — a company I created out of nothing with my mother, my father and my three siblings.”
Palmer previously said that Keke was just a family nickname.
“It went from just something that my family called me to being something that the whole world called me,” she told Glamour in 2022. “And more than anything, I would love to go back to Lauren.”

title: Nicolas Cage Reveals He Legally Changed His Name in 2025
summary: Nicolas Cage, born Nicolas Coppola, has revealed that he has legally changed his name.
story: Nicolas Cage is not just a stage name.
The Oscar-winning actor, who was born Nicolas Coppola and is the nephew of Francis Ford Coppola, revealed he legally changed his name to Nicolas Cage.

The name was originally inspired by the Marvel Comics character Luke Cage and the composer John Cage. Asked in an interview with Variety if it’s “strange” to still go by a stage name, the actor said, “No. I am Nick Cage.”
“I changed my name legally last year,” he said. “I’m Nick Cage in life, and I’m Nick Cage on camera.”
Cage said he prefers forging his own identity with his family rather than be on the outskirts of another.
“‘Tis better to be the patriarch of my own little family than the clown cousin on the margins of someone else’s, so I decided I’m going to bring it on and be ‘Cage,’” he said.
Reflecting on the origin of the name, Cage said he was looking for something “short and sweet,” like James Dean.

“‘Cage’ is a name that I liked coming across in the comics — I just thought he had a cool name — and I grew up in a very avant-garde, artsy family and there was talk about John Cage and the experimental compositions that he did,” the actor said.

“I thought, well, I’ll keep the name ‘Nicolas’ because my father named me Nicolas — with French spelling, which has always frustrated me, because everyone adds an ‘h.’ I don’t know why he gave me the French spelling! But he did,” he added.

Cage has been open about why he decided not to use the name Coppola during his career. In 1982’s “Fast Times at Ridgemont High,” he was credited as Nicolas Coppola, but people kept connecting him to his uncle and one of the famous lines from his movie “Apocalypse Now.”

“People would not stop saying things like, ‘I love the smell of Nicolas in the morning’ because of ‘Apocalypse Now,’ when Robert Duvall said, ‘I love the smell of napalm in the morning,’” Cage told Wired in 2022. “And it made it hard to work and I said, ‘I don’t need this.’”

Cage will make the jump to the small screen in his newest project, playing Ben Reilly/The Spider in “Spider-Noir,” now available on Prime Video. He previously voiced Spider-Man Noir in the 2018 movie “Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse.”
Cage will also star in the upcoming biopic of late NFL head coach and color commentator John Madden.

title: Jenna Bush Hager Says Her June 2026 Read With Jenna Pick Rivals ‘The Secret History’
summary: Jenna Bush Hager’s June 2026 book club pick is The Children by Melissa Albert.
story: Jenna Bush Hager compares her June 2026 book pick, “The Children” by Melissa Albert, to one of her all-time favorites.
“Not since Donna Tartt’s ‘Secret History’ have I loved a book filled with such magic and mystery as I have Melissa Albert’s ‘The Children,’” Jenna says.

The novel, out June 2, follows the two adult children of a famous children’s book novelist, Edith Sharpe. She died young, at 31, leaving behind a complicated legacy, one that ultimately leads to her children’s estrangement.
“Guinevere and Ennis have been separated as adults based on their childhood. Their mother, a famous children’s book author, has woven her real children into her stories,” Jenna says.
According to Jenna, the book asks the question: ‘What weight do our childhood memories hold?’”
"The Children" by Melissa Albert

Speaking to TODAY.com, Melissa Albert — author of the YA “Hazel Wood” series — says she the book came from a love of classics like “Peter Pan” by J.M. Barrie and “The Chronicles of Narnia” by C.S. Lewis.
“I’ve always continually revisited these books, and every time I read them again, they seem a little bit different,” she says.

For example, she learned many of the books were inspired by real-life children. “When I was a kid that seemed incredibly glamorous, and as I grew older it seemed the story was darker than I remembered,” she says.
Take Christopher Robin of “Winnie the Pooh,” based on the son of the books’ author A.A. Milne. Christopher Robin came to have an ambivalent relationship with the books.
“I read it as a kid, as an adult, as a writer and now, a mother. I feel like that was the final step I needed to take to see the story I wanted to write,” she says.
“The most deepest root for me is just loving these books. It’s such a celebration of portal fantasy — no matter how dark it got, I wanted it to be leavened by enchantment,” she continues.
While reading, she hopes people play with the idea of childhood memory.
“I hope they say, ‘Is this supernatural or is this a child’s view of the real world?’”

title: Gayle King Shares the 8 Words Oprah Winfrey Told Her After Her Husband Cheated
summary: Gayle King recounts Oprah Winfrey’s reaction to her husband having an affair and her reaction to him getting back together.
story: Gayle King and Oprah Winfrey’s friendship has spanned decades, creating an unbreakable bond that King leaned on when she went through a challenging moment in her life.
The “CBS Mornings” anchor appeared as a guest on the May 27 episode of the “Call Her Daddy” podcast and shared a detailed explanation about why her marriage with ex-husband William Bumpus ended.
During her conversation with host Alex Cooper, King revealed how she discovered Bumpus was cheating on her and how Winfrey, her dear friend of over 50 years, helped her process the infidelity.
In the episode, Cooper mentioned that Winfrey had an opinion about King’s ex. “Oh, Lord. Yes she did,” King replied before recalling the moment she learned about her husband’s affair.

King said she had an inkling something was wrong when she and her former spouse were playing tennis with another couple, who were their friends. She noticed there was “such an intimacy” in the way her friend spoke to Bumpus. But, she said when she mentioned the interaction to her then-husband later, he tried to “gaslight” her and quell her suspicions.

“That said, I still didn’t think anything,” King noted. “And Oprah always says, ‘Sometimes you get a whisper, and then when you ignore the whisper, it becomes a little pebble, and then you ignore the pebble. And next thing you know, it keeps going. Then you have a full-blown catastrophe.”
King’s catastrophic moment came when she returned home from a trip earlier than expected and found her husband cheating with the same woman they played tennis with.

King remembered arriving at her house with their two children — Kirby and William, Jr. — and thinking it was odd that the alarm was set. She said her husband then came “flying out of the room” with a towel on and told her she couldn’t come inside. She later found a woman, whom she and Cooper nicknamed “Casey,” “cowering behind the door in my towel.”
After Bumpus and the woman left, King immediately called Winfrey.
“I called Oprah. Oprah goes, ‘Wait, let me get this straight. He left you there alone and went with her?’” King shared.
Even though King wasn’t ready to face reality, Winfrey told her these eight words: “‘You don’t even know how bad this is.’”
King said “it was a good point,” but “at the time I couldn’t hear it.”
King said the two stayed on the phone for about two hours and that Winfrey was the only person she confided in.

“My mom is no longer with us. I was very close to my mom. I talked to my mom every single day, twice a day, (but didn’t tell her) because I didn’t want anybody to know. I got three sisters, didn’t tell them.” King continued. “The only person I told was Oprah.”
Winfrey told her to call the other woman’s husband and break the news to him. He initially didn’t believe King until she mentioned intimate details.
King and Bumpus, who married in 1982, divorced in 1993. The journalist revealed that the two briefly reconciled after their split, but she realized he had not changed and she didn’t trust him.

“He was cheating again. But now I had to go back with him because he was crying. He was apologizing, and I said, ‘But now he’ll be with somebody else, and then he’ll be all better,’” she recalled thinking before Winfrey stepped in to give her some tough love.
“Oprah goes, ‘No, he ain’t changed,’” King shared. “I said, ‘Oprah, you don’t understand, because you don’t have children. He was on his knees and he was crying.’ She goes, ‘Yeah, OK.’”
Finally, Winfrey had enough of King’s excuses.
“She got so sick of hearing (it). She goes, ‘Please go back with him. Please. I will drive the moving van. Please!’ That’s when you realize you’re telling people too much,” King added laughing.
But King said Winfrey was there for her and always listened to her relationship woes. She joked that Winfrey would now say that it felt like they went through the divorce together.
She also credited Winfrey for making her see that the situation was beyond repair. King shared that Winfrey asked her to fly to Chicago to take her mind off things.
“She goes, ‘I will send you tickets. You, Kirby and Will come to Chicago. Just come. Just get on a plane and leave,’” Winfrey told her.

King said that she needed to know the truth and that that realization helped lead her to the life she has now. She knew she needed to be with someone who was proud of her and didn’t feel threatened by her success.

“That will never happen to me again,” she said. “You want somebody who lifts you up. You want somebody who’s proud of you. You want somebody who thinks, ‘God, I think it’s great being with you.’ I’m looking for him.”

title: Damian Lewis Shocks TODAY Hosts With Story of How He ‘Made’ Timothée Chalamet
summary: Damian Lewis tells his story of how he helped discover Timothée Chalamet and send him on the road to stardom.
story: Timothée Chalamet owes his success to Damian Lewis — at least that’s how Lewis sees it.

Long before he was one of Hollywood’s biggest stars, Chalamet had a recurring role on the second season of “Homeland” in 2012. Lewis, of course, won an Emmy Award for his work on the show, but says he could tell Chalamet was something special when he first spotted him in North Carolina.

“I called my agent in L.A, and I said, ‘You got to come down and see this young dude who’s acting in this show. I think you’re going to love him, and I don’t think he has representation yet,’ etc. etc. etc,” Lewis told Craig Melvin, Dylan Dreyer and Savannah Sellers on the 3rd hour of TODAY May 27.
Lewis said his agent was impressed with Chalamet.
“And my agent of the time, Brian, came down, fell in love with him, said, ‘I think he’s got all the stuff. This is amazing,’” the “Band of Brothers” star said.

“And right at that moment, (he) had the rights to ‘Call Me by Your Name’ with his partner, a guy called Peter, and they said, ‘We just met this guy, Luca Guadagnino, maybe you’d like to meet him.’ And off he went into ‘Call Me by Your Name.’”

That Guadagnino-directed 2017 movie marked the first of Chalamet’s three Academy Award nominations for best actor and helped put him on the map. Lewis said he deserves his cut for helping to make Chalamet a star.
“I’m taking 15%,” he joked on TODAY.
A shocked Craig thought Lewis may have undersold the magnitude of the story.
“That wasn’t hyperbole at all,” he said.
“Can we say it here?” Lewis said.
“Yeah,” Craig replied.
“I made Chalamet!” Lewis said.
“You made Chalamet. Damian Lewis, it’s been verified,” Craig said.
Lewis and “Homeland” co-star Claire Danes reunited earlier this year at the Actor Awards, using the opportunity to joke that they’re responsible for Chalamet’s rise as an actor.

“We even had Timothée Chalamet on our show. Where do you think he learned how to handle the stress of a Safdie movie?” Danes joked, referring to Chalamet’s Oscar-nominated role in director Josh Safdie’s “Marty Supreme.”
“We made you, Timothée,” Lewis added.

title: The ‘Summer House’ Reunion Comment So ‘Harsh’ It Made Kyle Cooke Defend Ex Amanda Batula to Ciara Miller
summary: Kyle Cooke defended ex Amanda Batula after Ciara Miller said a comment about her being the perfect person for West Wilson because she is weak.
story: Kyle Cooke came to the defense of his ex-wife Amanda Batula as a tense moment unfolded during the “Summer House” Season 10 reunion.
The first episode of the three-part special, which aired May 26, saw cast members of the Bravo reality series weighing in on Amanda’s controversial relationship with West Wilson.

Amid ongoing dating rumors, the two castmates confirmed their romance in March. The timing of their relationship raised eyebrows among viewers and the couple’s co-stars since Amanda and Kyle announced that they were ending their marriage in January. In Season 10 of “Summer House,” which filmed last summer, West had also been rekindling his romance with Ciara Miller.

During the first episode of the reunion, Ciara expressed how betrayed she feels by Amanda and West and even shared text messages to prove that Amanda had lied to her about her budding relationship with West.
At one point, Ciara called Amanda, her former friend, a “f----ing snake.”

“There are a million other f----ing guys in New York City, but you chose the one — and you know how much that would f--- me up — you chose the one guy,” she said. “You did it on purpose because you’re a f----ing snake, Amanda. You’re a snake in the f----ing grass, is what you are.”

During the discussion, “Summer House” cast member Lindsay Hubbard suggested that Amanda and West won’t end up together. Ciara, meanwhile, had a theory they would work out. She said Amanda is the “best” type of woman for West.

“Someone who’s not going to check him on anything. That’s totally Amanda. She’s very mute. She’s going to be that weak figure that he needs, and he can always be the star in the relationship.” she said. “I actually think maybe it could work.”
Kyle interjected at this point and addressed Ciara. It’s worth noting that earlier on in the episode, before the reunion, the two appeared to bond and make a pact not to cry or storm out.
“That was a little harsh,” Kyle told Ciara.
Ciara offered up a spicy response, saying, “That’s your wife. She’s not my wife.”
At the beginning of the episode, Amanda said she was ready to “take accountability and apologize, especially to Ciara and Kyle.”
In response, Ciara said, “I’m glad you could say our name this time.”
Her comment was a reference to a joint statement Amanda and West issued in March to announce their relationship. The statement did not address Miller and Cooke specifically.
While discussing the origins of her relationship with West, Amanda said, “Sometimes you can’t help who you like and who you are attracted to.”
“You actually can help it,” Ciara said. “When you’re married you can help it.”
Amanda also confirmed that she and Kyle are not officially divorced yet.
The second part of the reunion airs on June 2 and the third on June 9. (Bravo is owned by NBCUniversal, TODAY’s parent company).

title: Everything Ciara, West and Amanda Say In ‘Summer House’ Reunion Part 1
summary: Here’s what Ciara Miller, West Wilson and Amanda Batula said to each other in Part 1 of the “Summer House” Season 10 reunion.
story: Bravo has aired Part 1 of the anticipated three-part “Summer House” Season 10 reunion, and Ciara Miller didn’t hold back when it came to how betrayed she feels by her co-stars Amanda Batula and West Wilson.

The explosive reunion was filmed April 23, less than a month after Amanda and West confirmed their relationship. This was a scandal that crippled the Bravoverse for its many layers: West is Ciara’s ex and Amanda is Ciara’s close friend; Amanda and her husband, fellow “Summer House” cast member Kyle Cooke, had just announced their split in January; viewers had also been watching West and Ciara rekindle their romance in new episodes of Season 10, which taped last summer.
Amanda and West explained in their joint public statement that their romantic “connection” was born out of a “genuine, longstanding friendship.”

“Summer House” cast members had since publicly shared their emotions about the scandal and what they did and didn’t know. Ciara gave her reaction in an interview with Glamour magazine, calling the news a “major mindf---.” West had spoken briefly in various episodes of his podcast, the most recent of which he said he and Kyle had three-hour conversation.
Amanda apologized in a statement shared to her Instagram story on April 10 without naming anyone specifically.
“I’m truly sorry to everyone I’ve disappointed and hurt, especially those I know personally who I’ve reached out to individually,” Amanda wrote.
She said she would be “addressing any and all questions honestly and directly” at the reunion.
Viewers had to patiently wait until May 26 to see how it all played out. Here’s what Amanda, West and Ciara had to say in Part 1 of the “Summer House” Season 10 reunion.
What Ciara Said at Part 1 of the ‘Summer House’ Reunion

A big chunk of this episode showed the “Summer House” cast preparing to take the reunion stage. Ciara was the first to receive a visit from host Andy Cohen, who had been making his rounds in the dressing areas.
She was visibly upset when she told Andy that Amanda was the person she felt most betrayed by. Her most pressing question for both Amanda and West was, “Why?”
When the cast took the reunion stage, Andy wasted no time addressing the scandal and asked Ciara when she suspected something was going on between Amanda and West.
Ciara gave a date, Jan. 17, when she texted Amanda to invite her to drinks with castmate Mia Calabrese and saw her phone’s location was at West’s house.
“I said, ‘Hm, that’s weird, why would she be at West’s house?’” Ciara said.
“Because West was there for me all summer,” Amanda said.
“Where was I?” Ciara said.
“Also there for me,” Amanda said.
“How many times have I asked you to hang out? Have I asked to come over to your house? Have I invited you to do stuff? And you act like your phone doesn’t work,” Ciara said.
Ciara clapped back at Amanda’s notion that West was fair to pursue.
“There are a million other f----ing guys in New York City, but you chose the one — and you know how much that would f--- me up — you chose the one guy,” she said.

“You did it on purpose because you’re a f----ing snake, Amanda. You’re a snake in the f----ing grass, is what you are. You’re a snake in the f----ing grass, and you should honestly just say OK because you know you are. You move silent, but you’re f----ing deadly.”

Ciara pulled out her phone to show texts that Amanda sent to Ciara the day that rumors first emerged online about Amanda and West. (These rumors circulated a few weeks before Amanda and West confirmed their relationship.)

Amanda’s text to Ciara said: “Hi, checking in. The internet is f----ing on one right now and I feel like everything I do is under a microscope and I can’t believe I’m even having to text you this right now but West and I are very much so just friends. I’m sad we’re even trying to discuss this.”
After Ciara read this text, Andy asked Amanda to clarify if she and West had been hooking up at that time. Amanda replied they had been “talking” and “everything that was happening” was “PG.”
Ciara read aloud her text back to Amanda: “So I can expect for there to be no photos or videos coming out? Feels like it’s such a widespread rumor for there to be no veracity to it.”

Amanda texted back: “Nothing different than what I’ve posted myself. People have just seen us with each other more and are being psychos. I hear what you’re saying, but I feel like I can’t even exist right now without people over analyzing everything I do.”
Ciara then texted back: “OK, so for clarity, since I don’t really feel like you’re making it clear, you and West haven’t hooked up, made out, held hands under their table, or had sex?”
Amanda then texted: “Correct.”
On the reunion stage, Amanda admitted, “I lied. I 100% lied.”

Ciara then read another text from Amanda that came a few days later: “Do you want to talk at all? I’m super in my head about where we left off this conversation, and I hate the thought of things being weird between us, because of Wes, like that’s so silly to me.”
When cast member Lindsay Hubbard suggested that Amanda and West will not end up together, Ciara countered that Amanda is the “best” type of woman for West.

“Someone who’s not going to check him on anything. That’s totally Amanda. She’s very mute. She’s going to be that weak figure that he needs, and he can always be the star in the relationship.” she said. “I actually think maybe it could work.”
Kyle then said to Ciara, “That was a little harsh.”
“That’s your wife. She’s not my wife,” Ciara replied.
What West Said at Part 1 of the ‘Summer House’ Reunion
At the reunion’s start, West told the group he wanted to clear up the “wildfire of what this became.”
Ciara accused West of lying about being in another relationship earlier this year.

“I think you’re the most fraudulent person sitting on this couch,” she told West. “We’ve been here three years now, and I don’t think anybody really truly knows who you are. You live a different life off camera than you do on camera. We don’t know what you’re doing, and probably the reason why it was so confusing is because you have a girlfriend: Meija.”
West said she was “not a girlfriend” but someone he was “seeing.”  Meija made him the horse-hair tie that West wore on “Watch What Happens Live” in March, he said.
Amanda said that she knew West was dating Meija at the time.
Cast member Jesse Solomon, who is West’s close friend, called both West and Amanda out for lying to him about their relationship when they had opportunities to tell the truth.

“I spoke with Amanda, I sat down with her for drinks, and she told me that it just wasn’t true,” Jesse said. “West, I don’t know, but I would text him asking, and he would tell me that it wasn’t true.”
West replied by owning up to lying.

“The lying part, I will not sit here and act like I didn’t do it,” he said. “I 100% lied to people here, and I take full accountability for that. It’s not cool to do in general, it’s not cool to do to your friends, and no matter the case, lying to people you care about is not OK.”
When Andy asked West and Amanda who made the first move, West said he voiced his feelings first.
“I kind of looked at Amanda, and I was like, ‘Am I my crazy, or is there a little something going on here?’”
He then said their first kiss was in February after a brunch. When asked when they first slept together, Amanda replied that it was after they released their public statement.
“Also to clear up — this isn’t a sex scandal. This was, like, hanging out,” West said.
Andy and multiple cast members asked West to address his mixed signals toward Ciara last summer, as seen on the show.

“I wouldn’t lie about a relationship being great, but I also recognized in that time I was not going to be what Ciara needed, and we ended it,” he said. “We got dinner right after summer, and I told Ciara what my plans were, and how much I learned the first time from what affected Ciara, and that I wasn’t going to just jump down that rabbit hole again.

I know a lot of things that can be casual for me are not casual for Ciara, and we had that conversation. And I’ve made plenty of mistakes along the way, but I was very proactive in trying to protect our friendship and not send wrong messages.”
Mia then asked West if he thought an emotional connection was equally as important as being physical.

“Obviously we didn’t talk for two years, and we were kind of like right back at it,” he said. “That is a real thing, and I fell short in those moments. I will acknowledge that. But there are numerous times where I tried to make steps to not allow us to get to a romantic place or a physical place and keep it as friends, because I wanted to be your friend.”
What Amanda Said at Part 1 of the ‘Summer House’ Reunion
When the reunion began, Amanda said she was prepared to “take accountability and apologize, especially to Ciara and Kyle.”
To that, Ciara replied, “I’m glad you could say our name this time” — a reference to the fact that Amanda and West’s joint statement did not address them specifically.
When Mia accused Amanda of prioritizing her relationship with West over her friendship with Ciara, Amanda defended herself.
“I had an awakening. I was living on my own. I had separated from my ex. I was trying to prioritize myself,” she said.
“I was also hanging out with other people. I was not just hanging out with West, OK? I was plenty of other friends where he was not present at all.”

The heart of the discussion was the extent of Amanda and West’s betrayal. When Andy asked the group to weigh in on Amanda and West’s joint statement revealing their relationship, West said there were “a lot of layers to it.”
“Is that enough layers to go, ‘What the f--- are we doing?’” cast member Carl Radke said.

“Right, it was so conscious that you guys talked about it,” Ciara said. “It wasn’t like a drunk hookup. You guys talked about it. You knew how dumb and how weird it would be, and you still did it. You chose to do it. You kept doing it. And you were hiding it.”
Amanda said, “Sometimes you can’t help who you like and who you are attracted to.”
“You actually can help it,” Ciara said. “When you’re married you can help it.”
Later, Amanda said what she “should have said” to Ciara when rumors about her and West first circulated online.
“Maybe I should have said from the get-go, I don’t know what’s going on, I’m having feelings for someone that I shouldn’t have feelings for, I don’t know what to do about it,” Amanda said.
“Then you remove yourself,” Ciara said.
“So I’m not allowed to pursue something that I feel strongly about?” Amanda replied.
“Well clearly you don’t feel strongly if you’re trying to understand it,” Kyle said.
“So no one in this world has ever been in a situation like this before?” Amanda asked.
“In high school maybe,” Lindsay said. “We’re in our 30s!”
“This is not the craziest thing that’s ever happened in the whole entire world,” Amanda said. “You guys are acting like I had an affair.”
Ciara and West kissed the last weekend of the summer, shown in finale episode of Season 10.
“Were you aware they were canoodling?” Andy asked Amanda.
“Not to that extent, no,” she said.

title: Joe Jonas Recalls ‘Bad Audition’ for This Popular Apple TV Show: ‘I Crash and Burn’
summary: Joe Jonas recalls his “bad audition” for the popular Apple TV show “The Morning Show.”
story: Joe Jonas is cringing over his crummy audition for the hit Apple TV series “The Morning Show.”

The Jonas Brothers singer recalled how he bombed his audition for the series, which stars Jennifer Aniston and Reese Witherspoon, while chatting with brothers Nick Jonas and Kevin Jonas on the May 26 episode of their “Hey Jonas!” podcast.
“I show up early and they’re like, ‘Oh.’ And they did this thing which was very kind, they were like, ‘Oh, we have a private room for you,’” Joe Jonas recalled.
The 36-year-old musician quickly realized he had been placed in an area where he could easily listen as other actors auditioned for the same role.
“This is the saddest room you can imagine,” he joked. “I could hear clear as day all the guys auditioning and these guys were crushing it.

“And I go in and I just crash and burn,” he said, not sharing the role he went out for. “... It was literally like, I don’t even know what I’m auditioning for... It was just gone for me. And it was such a bad audition.”
“Bad auditions happen,” Nick Jonas told his older brother before asking, “Would you say that’s your worst audition?”
“I think also ‘Wicked’ was a bit of a strange one,” Joe Jonas responded, “because there was, like, incredible theater vocalists I could hear clear as day.”
While speaking to Entertainment Weekly in 2025, the brothers teased Joe Jonas over his “terrible” audition for his starring role in the 2008 Disney movie musical “Camp Rock.”

“Joe got the role of Shane Gray in ‘Camp Rock’ as our band was already doing its thing for a couple years,” Nick Jonas recalled. “Joe gets this call to audition for this Disney movie, and I recall us helping him do his self-tape audition in our hotel room. Like a Holiday Inn in Albuquerque or something.”
“I wish we could find this footage,” Kevin Jonas interjected.
“Disney has it somewhere,” Joe Jonas responded. “In their vaults.”
The brothers have also competed against each other for roles.
Nick Jonas previously confirmed to Variety that both he and Joe Jonas went up for the role of Fiyero in “Wicked.”
“Joe and I both went out and we didn’t get it. But that’s the life of an actor,” he said of the part, which went to actor Jonathan Bailey.
In 2023, Joe Jonas recalled how Nick Jonas crushed his dreams when he was chosen over him for a coach role on “The Voice.”

“I remember — and I don’t even know if you know this, some more tea that hasn’t been spilled — it was at the most picturesque setting, it was at the Fleetwood Mac show at MSG (Madison Square Garden), and as they’re playing ‘Landslide,’ I found out that he was going to be a judge on ‘The Voice,’” Joe Jonas revealed on the May 22, 2023 episode of Dax Shepard’s “Armchair Expert” podcast.
“I was so jealous. I cried my eyes out to ‘Landslide,’” he joked.
“It was bittersweet, of course, because I’m super happy for him,” added the singer, who went on to be a judge on Season 7 of the Australian version of the show.
“But I’m also bummed because I want that f------ gig.”

title: BTS Is Releasing Limited-Edition Oreos: What’s the Flavor?
summary: Oreo and BTS announced they had joined forces (and their considerable fanbases) to launch the Limited Edition Oreo & BTS Cookies in a hotteok-inspired flavor.
story: When BTS sang, “One day it’ll be a good day, for sure,” they might have meant today.
On May 28, Oreo and BTS announced they had joined forces (and their considerable fanbases) to launch the Limited Edition Oreo & BTS Cookies.
Meant to emulate hotteok, the popular Korean street food, the brown sugar pancake-flavored Oreo cookie was curated by the seven-member group and dedicated to its fans.

The Limited Edition Oreo & BTS Cookies are filled with a sweet creme which remixes the flavors of hotteok, a fried stuffed pancake made with yeasted dough and typically filled with brown sugar, cinnamon and nuts.

“We took inspiration from it and explained to OREO what that flavor tastes like,” Jin said in a press release. He also called hotteok a “national snack” and said the cookie “really” tastes a lot like it. “We wanted to share with everyone the kinds of dessert flavors Koreans enjoy.”
“Yeah, it really has that flavor, so it’s fascinating,” J-Hope added in the release.
BTS said the whole band has “fond childhood memories” of eating both hotteok and Oreo cookies, so combining them seemed like a perfect fit.
